title: Densely-Packed Single-Server PIR for Variable-Length Data
author: Nakamori, Takuto
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Private Information Retrieval (PIR) enables a client to retrieve an item from a database without revealing which items were retrieved. Single-server PIR is attractive for its minimal infrastructure requirements. However, existing optimizations for variable-length records either add significant padding overhead or require complex key management, compromising security and simplicity. We propose a novel protocol that (1) packs variable-length entries as efficiently as symmetric-key schemes but without any additional key management, and (2) guarantees that each query retrieves at most one record with the strongest privacy guarantees. We implemented three protocols: the proposed method, a symmetric encryption method, and a padding method. We demonstrated that the proposed method provides a more stable performance than the padding method across various data length distributions. Furthermore, when the key transfer cost is included, the proposed method is up to 1.8 times faster than the symmetric encryption method.

We demonstrated that the proposed method provides a more stable performance than the padding method across various data length distributions. Furthermore, when the key transfer cost is included, the proposed method is up to 1.8 times faster than the symmetric encryption method.
